propositions: Leaf plans drift from their parent epic's declared scope with no criterion catching divergence until epic completion, so a new plan-review criterion is needed to gate the claim ticket-by-ticket.
G7 is a new AGENT-exec plan-review criterion that fires only on leaf tickets that have a parent (scope leaf AND require_parent_id), exempting parentless tickets, containers, bugs, and session_logs.

A leaf must be a subset of its parent's scope: a finding fires when the leaf adds out-of-parent deliverables, contradicts a parent AC, or redefines a parent deliverable, while consistent narrowing is not a finding; parent wins on conflict.
Routing requires a new require_parent_id boolean axis threaded via route_criteria into applies() and explicitly bool-validated in _validate_routing_entry, keeping validate_packaged_routing green.
G7's agent reads the parent at runtime via the always-available show_ticket tool using parent_id already in the ticket-graph context, adding no new context-assembly plumbing.

G7 ships advisory at 0.95 with a committed promotion-path doc containing a jq fire-rate query over plan_review_result_v1 sidecars and the 0.6-0.7 promotion band, with the actual promotion decision deferred.
